hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Medina is a sanctuary from that place to that. Its trees should not be cut and no heresy should be innovated nor any sin should be committed in it, and whoever innovates in it an heresy or commits sins (bad deeds), then he will incur the curse of Allah, the angels, and all the people." hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) came to Medina and ordered a mosque to be built and said, "O Bani Najjar! Suggest to me the price (of your land)." They said, "We do not want its price except from Allah" (i.e. they wished for a reward from Allah for giving up their land freely). So, the Prophet (ﷺ) ordered the graves of the pagans to be dug out and the land to be leveled, and the date-palm trees to be cut down. The cut datepalms were fixed in the direction of the Qibla of the mosque
question: What is the significance of offering land for the construction of a mosque without seeking monetary compensation?

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) told us a long narrative about Ad-Dajjal, and among the many things he mentioned, was his saying, "Ad-Dajjal will come and it will be forbidden for him to pass through the entrances of Medina. He will land in some of the salty barren areas (outside) Medina; on that day the best man or one of the best men will come up to him and say, 'I testify that you are the same Dajjal whose description was given to us by Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) .' Ad-Dajjal will say to the people, 'If I kill this man and bring him back to life again, will you doubt my claim?' They will say, 'No.' Then Ad-Dajjal will kill that man and bring him back to life. That man will say, 'Now I know your reality better than before.' Ad-Dajjal will say, 'I want to kill him but I cannot
question: What are the characteristics and actions of Ad-Dajjal as described in Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the characteristics and actions of Ad-Dajjal as mentioned in Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir:A bedouin came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and gave a pledge of allegiance for embracing Islam. The next day he came with fever and said (to the Prophet (ﷺ) ), "Please cancel my pledge (of embracing Islam and of emigrating to Medina)." The Prophet (ﷺ) refused (that request) three times and said, "Medina is like a furnace, it expels out the impurities (bad persons) and selects the good ones and makes them perfect
question: What is the significance of Medina in shaping the character of its inhabitants according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of Medina in shaping the character of its inhabitants by describing it as a furnace that expels impurities and selects the good ones.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:When the Prophet (ﷺ) went out for (the battle of) Uhud, some of his companions (hypocrites) returned (home). A party of the believers remarked that they would kill those (hypocrites) who had returned, but another party said that they would not kill them. So, this Divine Inspiration was revealed: "Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties concerning the hypocrites." (4.88) The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Medina expels the bad persons from it, as fire expels the impurities of iron
question: What was the reaction of the companions when some hypocrites returned home during the battle of Uhud?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the reaction of the companions and the subsequent revelation of the Quranic verse.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) reached Medina, Abu Bakr and Bilal became ill. When Abu Bakr's fever got worse, he would recite (this poetic verse): "Everybody is staying alive with his People, yet Death is nearer to him than His shoe laces." And Bilal, when his fever deserted him, would recite: "Would that I could stay overnight in A valley wherein I would be Surrounded by Idhkhir and Jalil (kinds of goodsmelling grass). Would that one day I could Drink the water of the Majanna, and Would that (The two mountains) Shama and Tafil would appear to me!" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Curse Shaiba bin Rabi`a and `Utba bin Rabi`a and Umaiya bin Khalaf as they turned us out of our land to the land of epidemics." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) then said, "O Allah! Make us love Medina as we love Mecca or even more than that. O Allah! Give blessings in our Sa and our Mudd (measures symbolizing food) and make the climate of Medina suitable for us, and divert its fever towards Al-juhfa." Aisha added: When we reached Medina, it was the most unhealthy of Allah's lands, and the valley of Buthan (the valley of Medina) used to flow with impure colored water
question: What challenges did the early Muslims face upon their arrival in Medina?
verification_reason: The text describes the challenges faced by the early Muslims upon their arrival in Medina, including illnesses and an unhealthy climate.

'Iraq will be conquered and some people will migrate (from Medina) and will urge their families and those who will obey them to migrate (to 'Iraq) although Medina will be better for them; if they but knew
question: What factors might influence people to migrate from a place of religious significance despite its virtues?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning factors that might influence people to migrate from Medina, such as the conquest of other regions and the desire to settle in those regions.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Verily, Belief returns and goes back to Medina as a snake returns and goes back to its hole (when in danger)
question: What analogy is used to describe the return of belief to Medina, and what does it imply about the nature of faith?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing the analogy used to describe the return of belief to Medina.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sa`d:I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying, "None plots against the people of Medina but that he will be dissolved (destroyed) like the salt is dissolved in water
question: How does the metaphor of salt dissolving in water relate to the fate of those who plot against Medina?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a metaphorical explanation.

hadith-eng: Narrated Usama:Once the Prophet (ﷺ) stood at the top of a (looked out from upon one) castle amongst the castles (or the high buildings) of Medina and said, "Do you see what I see? (No doubt) I see the spots where afflictions will take place among your houses (and these afflictions will be) as numerous as the spots where raindrops fall
question: What lessons can be drawn from the Prophet's warning about future afflictions in Medina?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing a prophetic warning about future afflictions in Medina.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Bakra:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "The terror caused by Al-Masih Ad-Dajjal will not enter Medina and at that time Medina will have seven gates and there will be two angels at each gate guarding them
question: What role do angels play in safeguarding Medina during the time of Al-Masih Ad-Dajjal?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the role of angels in safeguarding Medina during the time of Al-Masih Ad-Dajjal.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There are angels guarding the entrances (or roads) of Medina, neither plague nor Ad-Dajjal will be able to enter it
question: How is the city of Medina uniquely protected in Islamic eschatology?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about Medina's protection in Islamic eschatology.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There will be no town which Ad-Dajjal will not enter except Mecca and Medina, and there will be no entrance (road) (of both Mecca and Medina) but the angels will be standing in rows guarding it against him, and then Medina will shake with its inhabitants thrice (i.e. three earthquakes will take place) and Allah will expel all the non-believers and the hypocrites from it
question: What events are described to occur in Medina during the time of Ad-Dajjal's presence?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the events that will occur in Medina during the time of Ad-Dajjal's presence.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) told us a long narrative about Ad-Dajjal, and among the many things he mentioned, was his saying, "Ad-Dajjal will come and it will be forbidden for him to pass through the entrances of Medina. He will land in some of the salty barren areas (outside) Medina; on that day the best man or one of the best men will come up to him and say, 'I testify that you are the same Dajjal whose description was given to us by Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) .' Ad-Dajjal will say to the people, 'If I kill this man and bring him back to life again, will you doubt my claim?' They will say, 'No.' Then Ad-Dajjal will kill that man and bring him back to life. That man will say, 'Now I know your reality better than before.' Ad-Dajjal will say, 'I want to kill him but I cannot
question: What is the significance of the testimony of the best man regarding Ad-Dajjal's identity?
verification_reason: The answer text accurately describes the significance of the testimony of the best man regarding Ad-Dajjal's identity, as mentioned in the Hadith.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir:A bedouin came to the Prophet (ﷺ) and gave a pledge of allegiance for embracing Islam. The next day he came with fever and said (to the Prophet (ﷺ) ), "Please cancel my pledge (of embracing Islam and of emigrating to Medina)." The Prophet (ﷺ) refused (that request) three times and said, "Medina is like a furnace, it expels out the impurities (bad persons) and selects the good ones and makes them perfect
question: What lessons can be learned from the Prophet's response to the bedouin's request to cancel his pledge of allegiance?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear lesson from the Prophet's response to the bedouin's request.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:When the Prophet (ﷺ) went out for (the battle of) Uhud, some of his companions (hypocrites) returned (home). A party of the believers remarked that they would kill those (hypocrites) who had returned, but another party said that they would not kill them. So, this Divine Inspiration was revealed: "Then what is the matter with you that you are divided into two parties concerning the hypocrites." (4.88) The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Medina expels the bad persons from it, as fire expels the impurities of iron
question: What analogy did the Prophet (ﷺ) use to describe how Medina deals with bad persons?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the Prophet's statement about Medina expelling bad persons.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Bestow on Medina twice the blessings You bestowed on Mecca
question: What can be inferred about the Prophet's (ﷺ) affection for Medina from his prayer for its blessings?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by showing the Prophet's prayer for Medina's blessings.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:Whenever the Prophet (ﷺ) returned from a journey and observed the walls of Medina, he would make his Mount go fast, and if he was on an animal (i.e. a horse), he would make it gallop because of his love for Medina
question: What significance does Medina hold in the life of the Prophet (ﷺ) as demonstrated by his actions upon returning from travel?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the Prophet's actions upon returning to Medina, demonstrating his love and significance he holds for the city.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:(The people of) Bani Salama intended to shift near the mosque (of the Prophet) but Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) disliked to see Medina vacated and said, "O the people of Bani Salama! Don't you think that you will be rewarded for your footsteps which you take towards the mosque?" So, they stayed at their old places
question: How does the intention behind one's actions influence the reward in Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains how the intention behind one's actions (staying in their old places) influences the reward (being rewarded for their footsteps towards the mosque).

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There is a garden from the gardens of Paradise between my house and my pulpit, and my pulpit is on my Lake Fount (Al-Kauthar)
question: What is the importance of Al-Kauthar in Islamic eschatology?
verification_reason: The answer text directly addresses the question about Al-Kauthar's importance in Islamic eschatology.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) reached Medina, Abu Bakr and Bilal became ill. When Abu Bakr's fever got worse, he would recite (this poetic verse): "Everybody is staying alive with his People, yet Death is nearer to him than His shoe laces." And Bilal, when his fever deserted him, would recite: "Would that I could stay overnight in A valley wherein I would be Surrounded by Idhkhir and Jalil (kinds of goodsmelling grass). Would that one day I could Drink the water of the Majanna, and Would that (The two mountains) Shama and Tafil would appear to me!" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Allah! Curse Shaiba bin Rabi`a and `Utba bin Rabi`a and Umaiya bin Khalaf as they turned us out of our land to the land of epidemics." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) then said, "O Allah! Make us love Medina as we love Mecca or even more than that. O Allah! Give blessings in our Sa and our Mudd (measures symbolizing food) and make the climate of Medina suitable for us, and divert its fever towards Al-juhfa." Aisha added: When we reached Medina, it was the most unhealthy of Allah's lands, and the valley of Buthan (the valley of Medina) used to flow with impure colored water
question: What significance do the poetic verses recited by Abu Bakr and Bilal hold in the context of their illness?
verification_reason: The text provides a clear answer to the significance of the poetic verses recited by Abu Bakr and Bilal in the context of their illness, which is to express their acceptance and surrender to Allah's will.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) came to Medina and ordered a mosque to be built and said, "O Bani Najjar! Suggest to me the price (of your land)." They said, "We do not want its price except from Allah" (i.e. they wished for a reward from Allah for giving up their land freely). So, the Prophet (ﷺ) ordered the graves of the pagans to be dug out and the land to be leveled, and the date-palm trees to be cut down. The cut datepalms were fixed in the direction of the Qibla of the mosque
question: What was the purpose of using cut date-palm trees in the construction of the mosque's Qibla direction?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.
